transcript.text: Spruce and Parsley both wrap shared lib components in the same important outer pieces: `GlobalStyles`, `ErrorBoundary`, `AuthProvider`, `ProtectedRoute`, and app-specific providers. For the shared lib Storybook I’m narrowing that to the faithful shared runtime: global styles, auth context where relevant, routing context, and the toast provider that the lib’s interactive hooks/components expect.

transcript.text: The test utilities show real patterns I can reuse: router-backed rendering, Apollo `MockedProvider` for GraphQL component tests, and a toast context. I also found a mock toast decorator that is useful as a clue, but for the default preview I’m going to use the real LeafyGreen-backed `ToastProvider` so portal behavior is exercised instead of hidden.

transcript.text: I’ve got enough of the runtime shape now: shared stories need Emotion global styles, LeafyGreenProvider, the real toast provider, router context, Apollo wired to a real HTTP link, AuthProvider, deterministic localStorage/cookies/dates, and MSW handlers for Evergreen auth/login plus lib GraphQL queries. I’m going to put that into the shared preview and remove the starter “Example” stories so the Storybook surface is only real project code.

The preview is now the shared runtime: providers, global styles, seeded app-owned browser state, fixed dates, and MSW handlers live there. I’m adding the real stories now, starting with `Accordion` because it’s a small component with an interaction pattern copied directly from its tests and app usages.

I’m adding the smallest Storybook-test setup polyfill for that package compatibility, then I’ll re-run the Accordion story before moving on.

transcript.text: `IconWithTooltip` is rendering but its LeafyGreen hover trigger is not opening under browser-mode Vitest even when firing pointer and mouse events directly. Since the component’s behavior would require a story-specific workaround, I’m dropping that candidate and moving to components that prove the same portal/runtime setup without fighting the test harness.

`ExpiringAnnouncementTooltip` is next. This one uses the preview’s fixed date plus seeded cookies, so the stories can cover first view, recently seen, and expired states without putting cookie setup in the stories themselves.

The unused starter assets were binary, so I removed the obsolete `src/stories` directory directly after the patch tool refused to read them as UTF-8. Now I’m running the project’s real TypeScript check and then the full Storybook test/build sequence.

transcript.text:
Implemented the shared Storybook setup for `packages/lib`.

What changed:
- Built the default runtime in [.storybook/preview.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-20T10-26-41Z-ce9d614b/project/packages/lib/.storybook/preview.tsx): Emotion global styles, `MemoryRouter`, `ErrorBoundary`, `AuthProvider`, Apollo, `LeafyGreenProvider`, real `ToastProvider`, seeded `localStorage`/cookies, and fixed `MockDate`.
- Added global MSW setup in [.storybook/msw-handlers.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-20T10-26-41Z-ce9d614b/project/packages/lib/.storybook/msw-handlers.ts) plus `public/mockServiceWorker.js`.
- Added Storybook/Vitest browser polyfills for the Storybook/Emotion test path in [setupStorybookTests.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-20T10-26-41Z-ce9d614b/project/packages/lib/config/vitest/setupStorybookTests.ts).
- Removed the starter Storybook example stories/assets.
- Added 9 real story files with 27 total `play` tests:
  `Accordion`, `TaskStatusBadge`, `TestStatusBadge`, `Pagination`, `TreeSelect`, `Popconfirm`, `ExpiringAnnouncementTooltip`, `BaseTable`, and `LoginPage`.
- Included exactly one computed-style assertion, on `TaskStatusBadge`, to prove shared styling is loaded.

Verification:
- Ran each created story file with `npx vitest --project storybook <story-file>` as it was added.
- `pnpm check-types` passes.
- `npx vitest --project storybook` passes: 9 files, 27 tests.
- `npx storybook build` passes.

One note: I also tried `pnpm eslint:strict`; it is blocked by pre-existing repo issues in `.storybook/eval-support`, missing local GraphQL schema files under `sdlschema/**/*.graphql`, and `vitest.shims.d.ts` project inclusion. I fixed the lint issues from files I touched, but did not churn the eval-support artifacts or schema setup.

content.text: I’ll wire this the way the app itself runs, starting by tracing providers, CSS, browser state, portal usage, and data access from the package outward. I’ll inspect the current Storybook setup and candidate components first, then make the preview the shared runtime before adding stories and verifying each one.

content.text: Spruce and Parsley both wrap shared lib components in the same important outer pieces: `GlobalStyles`, `ErrorBoundary`, `AuthProvider`, `ProtectedRoute`, and app-specific providers. For the shared lib Storybook I’m narrowing that to the faithful shared runtime: global styles, auth context where relevant, routing context, and the toast provider that the lib’s interactive hooks/components expect.

content.text: The lockfile has `msw` only as an optional/transitive entry and no `msw-storybook-addon` or `mockdate`, so I’ll add the explicit Storybook testing dependencies after installing the workspace with the public npm registry override. That also gives us the browser-mode Vitest runner needed for the “run after each story” loop.

content.text: I’ve got enough of the runtime shape now: shared stories need Emotion global styles, LeafyGreenProvider, the real toast provider, router context, Apollo wired to a real HTTP link, AuthProvider, deterministic localStorage/cookies/dates, and MSW handlers for Evergreen auth/login plus lib GraphQL queries. I’m going to put that into the shared preview and remove the starter “Example” stories so the Storybook surface is only real project code.

content.text: The first story hit an environment issue before any story code ran: Storybook’s Vitest browser setup pulls in Emotion’s browser server bundle, which expects a `Buffer` global. I’m adding the smallest Storybook-test setup polyfill for that package compatibility, then I’ll re-run the Accordion story before moving on.

`Accordion` now passes in Storybook browser Vitest. I also found and fixed the browser-test polyfill gap in the Storybook/Vitest stack itself, so the remaining story files can exercise the shared preview instead of failing before render. Next up is the task badge story, including the single computed-style assertion that proves shared styling is loaded.

content.text: `Popconfirm` caught a real pattern issue: a controlled tooltip without a trigger is present but not visibly positioned, and using a `<button>` trigger creates nested buttons inside LeafyGreen’s tooltip trigger wrapper. I’m switching the stories to the span trigger pattern so the portal content is visible and valid, then rerunning.

Since the component’s behavior would require a story-specific workaround, I’m dropping that candidate and moving to components that prove the same portal/runtime setup without fighting the test harness.

content.text:
Implemented the shared Storybook setup for `packages/lib`.

What changed:
- Built the default runtime in [.storybook/preview.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-20T10-26-41Z-ce9d614b/project/packages/lib/.storybook/preview.tsx): Emotion global styles, `MemoryRouter`, `ErrorBoundary`, `AuthProvider`, Apollo, `LeafyGreenProvider`, real `ToastProvider`, seeded `localStorage`/cookies, and fixed `MockDate`.
- Added global MSW setup in [.storybook/msw-handlers.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-20T10-26-41Z-ce9d614b/project/packages/lib/.storybook/msw-handlers.ts) plus `public/mockServiceWorker.js`.
- Added Storybook/Vitest browser polyfills for the Storybook/Emotion test path in [setupStorybookTests.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-20T10-26-41Z-ce9d614b/project/packages/lib/config/vitest/setupStorybookTests.ts).
- Removed the starter Storybook example stories/assets.
- Added 9 real story files with 27 total `play` tests:
  `Accordion`, `TaskStatusBadge`, `TestStatusBadge`, `Pagination`, `TreeSelect`, `Popconfirm`, `ExpiringAnnouncementTooltip`, `BaseTable`, and `LoginPage`.
- Included exactly one computed-style assertion, on `TaskStatusBadge`, to prove shared styling is loaded.

Verification:
- Ran each created story file with `npx vitest --project storybook <story-file>` as it was added.
- `pnpm check-types` passes.
- `npx vitest --project storybook` passes: 9 files, 27 tests.
- `npx storybook build` passes.

One note: I also tried `pnpm eslint:strict`; it is blocked by pre-existing repo issues in `.storybook/eval-support`, missing local GraphQL schema files under `sdlschema/**/*.graphql`, and `vitest.shims.d.ts` project inclusion. I fixed the lint issues from files I touched, but did not churn the eval-support artifacts or schema setup.
